/**
46 
 * Utility functions that do not really belong to any class in particular.
47 
 */
48 
// @since 6.0 methods are no longer final
49 
public abstract class Utility {
50
51 
    /**
52 
     * Constructs a Utility.
53 
     *
54

55 
     */
56 
    public Utility() {
57 
        // Default constructor for subclasses
58 
    }

60 
    /**
61 
     * Decode characters into bytes. Used by <a href="Utility.html#decode(java.lang.String, boolean)">decode()</a>
62 
     */
63 
    private static final class JavaReader extends FilterReader {
64
65 
        JavaReader(final Reader in) {
66 
            super(in);
67 
        }
68
69 
        @Override
70 
        public int read() throws IOException {
71 
            final int b = in.read();
72 
            if (b != ESCAPE_CHAR) {
73 
                return b;
74 
            }
75 
            final int i = in.read();
76 
            if (i < 0) {
77 
                return -1;
78 
            }
79 
            if (i >= '0' && i <= '9' || i >= 'a' && i <= 'f') { // Normal escape
80 
                final int j = in.read();
81 
                if (j < 0) {
82 
                    return -1;
83 
                }
84 
                final char[] tmp = {(char) i, (char) j};
85 
                return Integer.parseInt(new String(tmp), 16);
86 
            }
87 
            return MAP_CHAR[i];
88 
        }
89
90 
        @Override
91 
        public int read(final char[] cbuf, final int off, final int len) throws IOException {
92 
            for (int i = 0; i < len; i++) {
93 
                cbuf[off + i] = (char) read();
94 
            }
95 
            return len;
96 
        }
97 
    }
98
99 
    /**
100 
     * Encode bytes into valid Java identifier characters. Used by
101 
     * <a href="Utility.html#encode(byte[], boolean)">encode()</a>
102 
     */
103 
    private static final class JavaWriter extends FilterWriter {
104
105 
        JavaWriter(final Writer out) {
106 
            super(out);
107 
        }
108
109 
        @Override
110 
        public void write(final char[] cbuf, final int off, final int len) throws IOException {
111 
            for (int i = 0; i < len; i++) {
112 
                write(cbuf[off + i]);
113 
            }
114 
        }
115
116 
        @Override
117 
        public void write(final int b) throws IOException {
118 
            if (isJavaIdentifierPart((char) b) && b != ESCAPE_CHAR) {
119 
                out.write(b);
120 
            } else {
121 
                out.write(ESCAPE_CHAR); // Escape character
122 
                // Special escape
123 
                if (b >= 0 && b < FREE_CHARS) {
124 
                    out.write(CHAR_MAP[b]);
125 
                } else { // Normal escape
126 
                    final char[] tmp = Integer.toHexString(b).toCharArray();
127 
                    if (tmp.length == 1) {
128 
                        out.write('0');
129 
                        out.write(tmp[0]);
130 
                    } else {
131 
                        out.write(tmp[0]);
132 
                        out.write(tmp[1]);
133 
                    }
134 
                }
135 
            }
136 
        }
137
138 
        @Override
139 
        public void write(final String str, final int off, final int len) throws IOException {
140 
            write(str.toCharArray(), off, len);
141 
        }
142 
    }
143
144 
    /*
145 
     * How many chars have been consumed during parsing in typeSignatureToString(). Read by methodSignatureToString(). Set
146 
     * by side effect, but only internally.
147 
     */
148 
    private static final ThreadLocal<Integer> CONSUMER_CHARS = ThreadLocal.withInitial(() -> Integer.valueOf(0));
149
150 
    /*
151 
     * The 'WIDE' instruction is used in the byte code to allow 16-bit wide indices for local variables. This opcode
152 
     * precedes an 'ILOAD', for example. The opcode immediately following takes an extra byte which is combined with the following
153 
     * byte to form a 16-bit value.
154 
     */
155 
    private static boolean wide;
156
157 
    // A-Z, g-z, _, $
158 
    private static final int FREE_CHARS = 48;
159
160 
    private static final int[] CHAR_MAP = new int[FREE_CHARS];
161
162 
    private static final int[] MAP_CHAR = new int[256]; // Reverse map
163
164 
    private static final char ESCAPE_CHAR = '$';
165
166 
    static {
167 
        int j = 0;
168 
        for (int i = 'A'; i <= 'Z'; i++) {
169 
            CHAR_MAP[j] = i;
170 
            MAP_CHAR[i] = j;
171 
            j++;
172 
        }
173 
        for (int i = 'g'; i <= 'z'; i++) {
174 
            CHAR_MAP[j] = i;
175 
            MAP_CHAR[i] = j;
176 
            j++;
177 
        }
178 
        CHAR_MAP[j] = '$';
179 
        MAP_CHAR['$'] = j;
180 
        j++;
181 
        CHAR_MAP[j] = '_';
182 
        MAP_CHAR['_'] = j;
183 
    }
184
185

191 
    public static String accessToString(final int accessFlags) {

193 
    }
194
195 
    /**
196 
     * Convert bit field of flags into string such as 'static final'.
197 
     * <p>
198 
     * Special case: Classes compiled with new compilers and with the 'ACC_SUPER' flag would be said to be "synchronized".
199 
     * This is because SUN used the same value for the flags 'ACC_SUPER' and 'ACC_SYNCHRONIZED'.
200 
     * </p>
201 
     *
202 
     * @param accessFlags Access flags.
203 
     * @param forClass access flags are for class qualifiers ?.
204 
     * @return String representation of flags.
205 
     */
206

207 
        final StringBuilder buf = new StringBuilder();
208 
        int p = 0;
209 3 1. accessToString : negated conditional → SURVIVED
2. accessToString : changed conditional boundary → SURVIVED
3. accessToString : Changed increment from 1 to -1 → SURVIVED

210 
            p = pow2(i);
211 2 1. accessToString : negated conditional → SURVIVED
2. accessToString : Replaced bitwise AND with OR → KILLED
 
            if ((accessFlags & p) != 0) {
212 
                /*
213 
                 * Special case: Classes compiled with new compilers and with the 'ACC_SUPER' flag would be said to be "synchronized".
214 
                 * This is because SUN used the same value for the flags 'ACC_SUPER' and 'ACC_SYNCHRONIZED'.
215 
                 */
216 3 1. accessToString : negated conditional → SURVIVED
2. accessToString : negated conditional → SURVIVED
3. accessToString : negated conditional → SURVIVED
 
                if (forClass && (p == Const.ACC_SUPER || p == Const.ACC_INTERFACE)) {
217 
                    continue;
218 
                }
219 
                buf.append(Const.getAccessName(i)).append(" ");
220 
            }
221 
        }
222 1 1. accessToString : replaced return value with "" for org/apache/bcel/classfile/Utility::accessToString → SURVIVED
 
        return buf.toString().trim();
223 
    }

    /**
226 
     * Convert (signed) byte to (unsigned) short value, that is, all negative values become positive.
227 
     */
228 
    private static short byteToShort(final byte b) {
229 4 1. byteToShort : Replaced integer addition with subtraction → SURVIVED
2. byteToShort : changed conditional boundary → SURVIVED
3. byteToShort : replaced short return with 0 for org/apache/bcel/classfile/Utility::byteToShort → SURVIVED
4. byteToShort : negated conditional → SURVIVED
 
        return b < 0 ? (short) (256 + b) : (short) b;
230 
    }

    /**
233 
     * Gets the class or interface type name.
234 
     *
235 
     * @param accessFlags the class flags.
236

237

238 
    public static String classOrInterface(final int accessFlags) {
239 3 1. classOrInterface : negated conditional → SURVIVED
2. classOrInterface : Replaced bitwise AND with OR → SURVIVED
3. classOrInterface : replaced return value with "" for org/apache/bcel/classfile/Utility::classOrInterface → SURVIVED
 
        return (accessFlags & Const.ACC_INTERFACE) != 0 ? "interface" : "class";
240 
    }

    /**
243 
     * Clears a bit in a flag.
244 
     *
245 
     * @param flag the flag value.
246 
     * @param i the bit position.
247 
     * @return 'flag' with bit 'i' set to 0.
248 
     */
249 
    public static int clearBit(final int flag, final int i) {
250 
        final int bit = pow2(i);
251 4 1. clearBit : Replaced XOR with AND → KILLED
2. clearBit : replaced int return with 0 for org/apache/bcel/classfile/Utility::clearBit → KILLED
3. clearBit : negated conditional → KILLED
4. clearBit : Replaced bitwise AND with OR → KILLED
 
        return (flag & bit) == 0 ? flag : flag ^ bit;
252 
    }

    /**
309 
     * Disassemble a stream of byte codes and return the string representation.
310 
     *
311 
     * @param bytes stream of bytes.
312 
     * @param constantPool Array of constants.
313 
     * @param verbose be verbose, for example print constant pool index.
314 
     * @return String representation of byte code.
315 
     * @throws IOException if a failure from reading from the bytes argument occurs
316 
     */
317 
    public static String codeToString(final ByteSequence bytes, final ConstantPool constantPool, final boolean verbose) throws IOException {
318 
        final short opcode = (short) bytes.readUnsignedByte();
319 
        int defaultOffset = 0;
320 
        final int low;
321 
        final int high;
322 
        final int npairs;
323 
        final int index;
324 
        final int vindex;
325 
        final int constant;
326 
        final int[] match;
327 
        final int[] jumpTable;
328 
        int noPadBytes = 0;
329 
        final int offset;
330 
        final StringBuilder buf = new StringBuilder(Const.getOpcodeName(opcode));
331 
        /*
332 
         * Special case: Skip (0-3) padding bytes, that is, the following bytes are 4-byte-aligned
333 
         */
334 2 1. codeToString : negated conditional → KILLED
2. codeToString : negated conditional → KILLED

335 1 1. codeToString : Replaced integer modulus with multiplication → TIMED_OUT
 
            final int remainder = bytes.getIndex() % 4;
336 2 1. codeToString : negated conditional → TIMED_OUT
2. codeToString : Replaced integer subtraction with addition → KILLED

337 2 1. codeToString : negated conditional → TIMED_OUT
2. codeToString : changed conditional boundary → KILLED

338 
                final byte b;
339 1 1. codeToString : negated conditional → SURVIVED

340 1 1. codeToString : removed call to java/io/PrintStream::println → NO_COVERAGE

341 
                }
342

343

344 
            defaultOffset = bytes.readInt();
345 
        }
346 
        switch (opcode) {
347 
        /*
348 
         * Table switch has variable length arguments.
349 
         */
350 
        case Const.TABLESWITCH:
351 
            low = bytes.readInt();
352 
            high = bytes.readInt();
353 3 1. codeToString : Replaced integer subtraction with addition → SURVIVED
2. codeToString : Replaced integer subtraction with addition → SURVIVED
3. codeToString : Replaced integer subtraction with addition → SURVIVED

354 1 1. codeToString : Replaced integer addition with subtraction → SURVIVED
 
            defaultOffset += offset;
355

2. codeToString : Replaced integer addition with subtraction → SURVIVED

358 1 1. codeToString : Replaced integer addition with subtraction → SURVIVED

360 3 1. codeToString : negated conditional → SURVIVED
2. codeToString : changed conditional boundary → SURVIVED
3. codeToString : Replaced integer subtraction with addition → SURVIVED

361

362

363

365 
            break;
366 
        /*
367 
         * Lookup switch has variable length arguments.
368 
         */
369 
        case Const.LOOKUPSWITCH: {
370 
            npairs = bytes.readInt();

2. codeToString : Replaced integer subtraction with addition → SURVIVED
3. codeToString : Replaced integer subtraction with addition → SURVIVED

                if (i < npairs - 1) {

        }
386 
            break;
387 
        /*
388 
         * Two address bytes + offset from start of byte stream form the jump target
389 
         */
390 
        case Const.GOTO:
391 
        case Const.IFEQ:
392 
        case Const.IFGE:
393 
        case Const.IFGT:
394 
        case Const.IFLE:
395 
        case Const.IFLT:
396

            buf.append("\t\t#").append(bytes.getIndex() - 1 + bytes.readInt());
416 
            break;
417

419

420 
        case Const.ALOAD:
421

423 
        case Const.DSTORE:
424 
        case Const.FLOAD:
425 
        case Const.FSTORE:
426

428

429 
        case Const.LSTORE:
430 
        case Const.RET:

            if (wide) {
432

433

434 
            } else {
435 
                vindex = bytes.readUnsignedByte();
436 
            }
437 
            buf.append("\t\t%").append(vindex);
438 
            break;
439 
        /*
440 
         * Remember wide byte which is used to form a 16-bit address in the following instruction. Relies on that the method is
441 
         * called again with the following opcode.
442 
         */
443 
        case Const.WIDE:
444

            buf.append("\t(wide)");
446 
            break;
447 
        /*
448 
         * Array of basic type.
449 
         */
450 
        case Const.NEWARRAY:
451

452 
            break;
453 
        /*
454 
         * Access object/class fields.
455 
         */
456 
        case Const.GETFIELD:
457

459

462 
            break;
463

464

465 
         */
466

467

469

470 1 1. codeToString : negated conditional → SURVIVED

471

474 1 1. codeToString : negated conditional → SURVIVED
 
            buf.append("\t<").append(constantPool.constantToString(index, Const.CONSTANT_Class)).append(">").append(verbose ? " (" + index + ")" : "");
475 
            break;
476 
        /*
477 
         * Operands are references to methods in constant pool
478 
         */
479 
        case Const.INVOKESPECIAL:
480 
        case Const.INVOKESTATIC:

            index = bytes.readUnsignedShort();

            final Constant c = constantPool.getConstant(index);

487

787

788 
     *      complex. Signatures are used to encode declarations written in the Java programming language that use types
789 
     *      outside the type system of the Java Virtual Machine. They are used to describe the type of any class, interface,
790

791

792

795

796
